## Overview

**v0.5.0-rc4 — first release where every matrix entry (Windows / macOS / Linux × archs / formats) successfully uploads.**

`rc1` / `rc2` / `rc3` each failed the GitHub Actions release workflow at
a different step, and each fix is now part of the workflow:

- `rc1` — *Sanity checks* compared `${TAG#v}=0.5.0-rc1` against
  `build.zig`'s `tildaz_version="0.5.0"`. Fix: strip `-rc*` / `-beta*` /
  `-alpha*` from the tag before comparing.
- `rc2` — *Sanity checks* also requires `dist/release-notes/v<tag>.md`
  to exist on the tagged commit. Fix: ship a per-RC notes file.
- `rc3` — *Verify artifacts* expected file names with the full tag
  (`tildaz-v0.5.0-rc3-...`) but `package.sh` outputs only the base
  version (`tildaz-v0.5.0-...`). Fix: the verify / upload steps now
  compute `BASE_VER` the same way the sanity check does. The aarch64
  / rpm job also failed in *Build + package* with `No compatible
  architectures found for build` because the Ubuntu `rpm` package
  does not include a platform entry for `aarch64-linux`. Fix:
  `package.sh` now passes `_target_cpu` / `_target_os` /
  `_target_platform` to `rpmbuild` so the platform lookup is bypassed.
- `rc4` (this release) — first time the workflow runs end-to-end and
  uploads all 11 artifacts.

Artifact file names use the base version (`tildaz-v0.5.0-...`)
regardless of the RC suffix. The GitHub Release tag page is what tells
you which RC the files belong to.

The Linux artifacts in this release are the first ever produced by the
official release workflow. Treat them as preview quality: KDE Plasma
6 and Cinnamon Wayland are verified by the maintainer; sway / Hyprland
/ Wayfire are untested; GNOME Wayland is limited support (no true
drop-down path).

Below is the same release content as `rc1` (no Linux feature changes
between RCs — only workflow fixes).

---

## rc1 release scope (carried into rc3)

**v0.5.0-rc1 — first Linux Wayland preview, plus Windows ARM64 and macOS / Windows ligature parity.**

This is the first release candidate for the Linux Wayland backend. It is a
preview: feedback is welcome, but production use is not yet recommended on
desktops other than KDE Plasma 6 and Cinnamon Wayland, which are the two
desktops the maintainer has tested against.

Compared to v0.4.3 (Windows + macOS), v0.5.0-rc1 adds:

- **Linux Wayland host** — direct Wayland client (no GTK / Qt dependency)
  with `xdg-shell` and `wlr-layer-shell` support, `wp_fractional_scale_v1`
  and `wp_viewporter` for KDE-style fractional scaling, software `wl_shm`
  renderer (placeholder until a final GPU path lands), fontconfig +
  FreeType + HarfBuzz fonts, `zwp_text_input_v3` IME, XDG Desktop Portal
  `GlobalShortcuts` + Unix-socket IPC fallback, full drop-down workflow on
  layer-shell compositors, KDE/Cinnamon/GNOME-portal hotkey integration,
  and four packaging formats (`.tar.gz`, `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage`) for
  both `x86_64` and `aarch64`.
- **Windows ARM64** — first official ARM64 release with bundled ConPTY for
  ARM64. Cross-built from the x86_64 GitHub runner.
- **Ligature parity across platforms** — Windows / macOS now share the
  cross-platform `ligature.classify` module that Linux already used:
  Fira Code "spacer" sequences (`=>` rendered as two glyphs that visually
  combine) render correctly, three-character ligatures (`===`, `<=>`,
  `||=`) look up via a shared `ligatureTriple` cache, and the GPOS
  `x_offset` unit fix repairs the visible gap previously seen on `||=`.

No config schema changes. macOS / Windows users see the same config as
v0.4.3.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 x64 | `tildaz-v0.5.0-rc1-win-x64.zip` | Unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. Unsigned — same SmartScreen / AV handling as v0.4.3. |
| Windows 11 ARM64 | `tildaz-v0.5.0-rc1-win-arm64.zip` | First-class ARM64 binary with ARM64-native ConPTY. Unsigned. |
| macOS 13+ universal | `tildaz-v0.5.0-rc1-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. Same ad-hoc-signing flow as v0.4.3. |
| Linux portable (any glibc 2.28+) | `tildaz-v0.5.0-rc1-linux-{x86_64,aarch64}.tar.gz` | Extract, then `./install.sh` writes the `.desktop` and icon under `~/.local/share/`. The binary stays in the extracted directory by default. No `sudo` required. |
| Linux Debian / Ubuntu | `tildaz_0.5.0-rc1_{amd64,arm64}.deb` | `sudo dpkg -i tildaz_*.deb`, or open with the Software app. |
| Linux Fedora / RHEL / openSUSE | `tildaz-0.5.0-rc1-1.{x86_64,aarch64}.rpm` | `sudo dnf install ./tildaz-*.rpm` (or `rpm -Uvh`). |
| Linux distro-independent | `TildaZ-0.5.0-rc1-{x86_64,aarch64}.AppImage` | `chmod +x TildaZ-*.AppImage && ./TildaZ-*.AppImage`. FUSE optional — runs on any glibc 2.28+ system. |

Linux binaries link against `glibc 2.28+` only at build time. Native
dependencies — `libwayland-client`, `libxkbcommon`, `libfreetype`,
`libfontconfig`, `libharfbuzz`, `libdbus-1` — are all loaded via runtime
`dlopen`, so missing libraries surface as targeted error dialogs rather
than load-time crashes.

## Migration from v0.4.3

None. The config schema is unchanged. Linux uses the same `config.json` at
`~/.config/tildaz/config.json`; default values for `shell`, `font.family`,
and `font.glyph_fallback` are Linux-specific (`/bin/bash`, `DejaVu Sans
Mono`, `Noto Sans CJK`, `Noto Color Emoji`).

## What's new since v0.4.3

### Linux Wayland — first preview ([#189](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/189))

The Linux host is implemented directly against the Wayland wire protocol
with no toolkit dependency. Capability is probed at startup and logged;
the host degrades cleanly when a compositor does not advertise a feature.

**Verified environments:**

- KDE Plasma 6.6.5 + KWin (Wayland) + xdg-desktop-portal-kde 6.6.5,
  including fractional scaling (125% / 150% / 170%).
- Cinnamon Wayland (mutter) + xdg-desktop-portal-gtk + fcitx5-hangul.

**Window / display.** `wlr-layer-shell` is used when advertised — TildaZ
becomes a real Quake-style drop-down with `keyboard_interactivity=exclusive`.
Compositors without `wlr-layer-shell` (e.g. GNOME mutter) fall back to a
normal `xdg-shell` toplevel window. `dock_position`, `width_percent`,
`height_percent`, `offset_percent`, `opacity_percent`, and `theme` from
`config.json` are honored. Live fractional scaling separates logical /
physical pixel coordinates and updates font + UI metrics on
`preferred_scale` events.

**Input.** Mouse selection drag, double-click word selection, wheel
scroll, scrollbar click-and-drag, automatic copy on selection finish,
right-click paste, `Ctrl+Shift+C` / `Ctrl+Shift+V`, `Shift+PgUp` /
`Shift+PgDn`, `Alt+F4` (with multi-tab confirm dialog), and `Alt+1..9`
tab index jump all work. Client-side key repeat follows
`wl_keyboard.repeat_info`. The OS cursor shape (`wp_cursor_shape_v1`) is
I-beam in the cell grid and rename text and arrow elsewhere — matching
Windows / macOS.

**Tabs.** Full multi-session tab UI: tab bar with arrow `<` / `>`, plus
`+`, close `x`, drag reorder with auto-scroll, double-click rename, IME
input during rename, 32-tab cap with dialog. Single-tab mode now hides
the tab bar entirely so the cell grid expands — matching Windows / macOS
([#127](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/127)).

**Fonts.** `fontconfig` discovery, `FreeType` rasterization (including
BGRA color emoji), and `HarfBuzz` shaping with two-character and
three-character ligature lookahead plus a grapheme cluster path for
combining marks, variation selectors, skin-tone modifiers, and ZWJ
sequences. Block-element / shade characters use procedural rectangles
that align cleanly across cells.

**IME.** `zwp_text_input_v3` with the spec's `done` / `apply` batch
pattern. Hangul / kanji / hanzi commit reaches the PTY; pre-edit is
drawn inline at the cursor with the purple background that matches
macOS / Windows; `Ctrl+key` during composition discards the pre-edit;
`set_cursor_rectangle` anchors the candidate popup; `set_content_type`
hints `purpose=terminal`. Tab rename inherits the same pre-edit
path. Tested with fcitx5-hangul.

**Global hotkey.** XDG Desktop Portal `GlobalShortcuts` is the primary
path. When the configured hotkey collides with an existing KDE binding,
TildaZ now detects the owner via `org.kde.KGlobalAccel`, asks the user
to confirm takeover via a Wayland modal dialog, and reassigns the key
via `setForeignShortcut` — config is the source of truth
([#207](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/207)). On desktops
without a `GlobalShortcuts` portal implementation, a second TildaZ
instance launched as `tildaz --toggle` connects to a Unix domain socket
at `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/tildaz.sock` and triggers hide / show on the
running instance — users can bind that command in their DE's keyboard
shortcuts panel ([#198](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/198)).

**Dialogs.** A native Wayland dialog backend renders TildaZ's
`showInfo` / `showError` / `showFatal` / `showConfirm` as scale-aware
layer-shell overlays with the same content shape used on Windows
(`MessageBoxW`) and macOS (`NSAlert`)
([#203](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/203)).

**Packaging.** `zig build package -Dtarget=...-linux-gnu.2.28
-Dformat={tar.gz,deb,rpm,AppImage}` produces all four artifacts. The
`install.sh` shipped in the `tar.gz` installs the `.desktop` template,
substitutes the executable path, copies the SVG icon under
`~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/scalable/`, and best-effort refreshes the
desktop database and icon cache. `auto_start=true` writes
`~/.config/autostart/tildaz.desktop` ([#189
L11](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/189),
[#202](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/202)).

**Known Linux limitations.**

- The current renderer is a software `wl_shm` placeholder. A final
  EGL / OpenGL renderer is planned but not in this preview. Subjective
  performance is fine for normal typing and shell output on the
  maintainer's hardware; we have not yet measured large bulk-output
  workloads on Linux.
- GNOME Wayland is **limited support** — `xdg-shell` toplevel window
  only. There is no compositor-blessed path for a true drop-down /
  always-on-top window in GNOME today. The terminal is usable; the
  drop-down workflow is not.
- sway, Hyprland, Wayfire, and other wlroots compositors are
  **untested** in this preview. The layer-shell path should work, but
  TildaZ has not yet been run against them with a maintainer-driven
  validation checklist.
- KDE Plasma's *floating dock* mode (the dock that hovers slightly above
  the screen edge) is partially overlapped by the layer-shell surface.
  Either use anchored dock mode (KDE Plasma's "Floating" toggle off) or
  use an integer scale (100% / 200%) to avoid sub-pixel rounding
  artifacts. See [#206](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/206).
- Z-order yield on focus loss (Windows / macOS lower their own window
  level so other apps come on top while staying visible behind) is
  **not implemented on Linux**. `wp_layer_shell_v1` only exposes four
  categorical layers (`background` / `bottom` / `top` / `overlay`) with
  no normal-window slot to mix into. Standard pattern for Linux
  drop-down terminals (yakuake / guake / Tilda). See
  [#195](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/195).
- When VSCode (Electron, X11 frontend) takes focus and then TildaZ
  takes focus back, fcitx5-hangul Hangul input may stop working until
  you focus a Wayland-frontend app once. This reproduces in `foot` and
  is a fcitx5 X11/Wayland frontend separation limitation, not a TildaZ
  bug. See [#194](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/194).
- Hanja conversion of already-committed Hangul (selecting committed
  Korean text and pressing the Hanja key to get a candidate popup) is
  **not supported** on Linux. The `zwp_text_input_v3` wire protocol has
  no reconversion request for the client to forward to the IME. Hanja
  conversion of *in-progress* Hangul (during pre-edit) works because
  fcitx5 / ibus handle that themselves. See
  [#209](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/209) (closed as a
  platform limitation).
- The hotkey accepts ASCII letters, digits, function keys, space, the
  backtick (`grave`), Tab, Return / Enter, Escape. Other ASCII symbols
  (`~`, `=`, `-`, `+`, etc.) are now rejected at parse time with a
  fatal dialog rather than silently falling back to F1
  ([#208](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/208)). Wider symbol
  support depends on portal-kde's `XdgShortcut::parse` mapping.

### Windows ARM64 ([#191](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/191))

The Windows release matrix now produces a native ARM64 build with a
bundled ARM64 ConPTY (`OpenConsole.exe` + `conpty.dll`). The build runs
on the same `windows-2022` x86_64 runner as before; Zig cross-compiles
PE/COFF for both architectures and selects the matching ConPTY bundle
at build time. ARM64 hardware smoke verification is still outstanding
and tracked in [#191](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/191).

### Cross-platform polish

- **OS cursor shape** ([#193](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/193))
  — Windows `WM_SETCURSOR`, macOS `NSCursor.IBeamCursor` via
  `resetCursorRects`, Linux `wp_cursor_shape_v1`. I-beam in the cell
  grid and active rename text; arrow on the tab bar, close `x`,
  scrollbar, and padding.
- **Z-order yield on focus loss for Windows / macOS**
  ([#195](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/195))
  — `WM_ACTIVATEAPP wParam=0` lowers to `HWND_NOTOPMOST` on Windows;
  `applicationDidResignActive:` sets `NSNormalWindowLevel` on macOS.
  The TildaZ window stays visible behind whichever app the user just
  activated rather than auto-hiding.
- **Single-tab cell sync on Linux** ([#127](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/127))
  — `Renderer.tabBarHeightPx(tab_count)` returns 0 when count < 2,
  and tab-count transitions call `ensureSessionGrid()` so all tabs'
  cols / rows are recomputed. Matches Windows
  `effectiveTabBarHeight()` and macOS `syncTerminalGeometry`.
- **Cross-platform ligature parity** ([#189](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/189))
  — Linux's `ligature.classify` / `ligaturePair` / `ligatureTriple`
  modules are now shared by Windows and macOS, giving consistent
  rendering of Fira Code `=>` `==` `!=` `<=` `>=` `&&` `||` `===`
  `!==` `<=>` `-->` `<--` `<==` `==>` `||=` across all three hosts.
- **Ligature spacer GPOS `x_offset` unit fix**
  — three independent host code paths previously read the GPOS
  `x_offset` in the wrong units, surfacing as a visible gap on
  `||=` in Fira Code. Fixed on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
- **macOS Hanja candidate UX polish** ([#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190))
  — follow-up fixes after the v0.4.3 reconversion landing.
- **macOS PTY child SIGTERM safety** ([#180](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/180))
  — already-exited child PIDs are skipped when shutting down so the
  parent does not signal a recycled PID.
- **Windows ConPTY environment buffer dynamic sizing**
  ([#184](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/184))
  — restoring the original environment after spawn no longer truncates
  large parent environments.
- **`scrollActive` page direction sign bug fix** — `Shift+PgUp` and
  `Shift+PgDn` were swapped on all three hosts due to a single sign
  error in the shared scroll module.

### Documentation

- `SPEC.md §7.1` rewritten as the canonical hotkey reference with
  explicit modifier aliases (`meta` / `option` / `command` / `logo` /
  `super` / `win` / `cmd`), an example table, a key-token table with
  acceptance notes, and the new hierarchical fallback chain for
  config ↔ system binding mismatches.
- `LINUX.md` updated to reflect every milestone status and known
  limitation through 2026-05-28.
- `README.md` install table extended to cover Linux artifacts.

## Build

```bash
zig build                                                 # native host
zig build -Dtarget=x86_64-linux-gnu.2.28                  # cross-build linux
zig build -Dtarget=aarch64-windows                        # cross-build win arm64
zig build package -Dtarget=x86_64-linux-gnu.2.28 -Dformat=AppImage
zig build test
```

Reproducible from `v0.5.0-rc1` with Zig 0.15.2. Dependencies in
`build.zig.zon` are pinned to 40-character commit SHA tarball URLs;
the release workflow rejects rolling references.

## Release candidate scope

This is `rc1` because the Linux backend is new. Bugs against Linux are
the highest signal for the next iteration. Windows and macOS changes in
this release are smaller and should be safe to use as-is — the cross-
platform ligature work and `scrollActive` direction fix are the only
behavior changes on those hosts.

Please file Linux feedback as new issues with the desktop / compositor
name, version, and a copy of `~/.local/state/tildaz/tildaz.log`.
